subject: ap-chemistry
title: Electrochemistry & Electrolysis
stimulus:
Sterling silver is an alloy that is commonly used to make jewelry and consists of 92.5% silver and 7.5% other metals, such as copper, by mass. Over time, the alloy can form a tarnish of $Ag_2S$(s) when it reacts with hydrogen sulfide, as represented by the following equation.

2 Ag(s) + $H_2S$(g) → $Ag_2S$(s) + $H_2$(g)

parts.prompt:
What are the oxidation numbers of silver in Ag(s) and $Ag_2S$(s)?

Ag(s) __________ $Ag_2S$(s) __________

The following table contains the atomic radii for silver and copper.

| Element | Silver (Ag) | Copper (Cu) |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| Atomic radius (pm) | 165 | 145 |

(i) Explain why sterling silver is better classified as a substitutional alloy than as an interstitial alloy.

(ii) Using principles of atomic structure and Coulomb's law, explain why silver has a larger atomic radius than copper does.

The $Ag_2S$ tarnish on sterling silver can be removed until only sterling silver remains. A student weighs a tarnished sterling silver sample both before and after removing the $Ag_2S$(s) (molar mass 247.80 g/mol) and records the data in the following table.

| | Before Tarnish Removal | After Tarnish Removal |
|-|------------------------|----------------------|
| Mass | 409.21 g | 398.94 g |

alternatives.correct_example: Silver and copper have similar radii, so the alloy would be substitutional versus
Silver has more occupied electron shells (n = 5) than copper (n = 4); the electrons in

parts.prompt:
Assuming that only $Ag_2S$(s) is removed, calculate the number of moles of silver atoms removed.

Rhodium plating is a process used to protect sterling silver from tarnishing. This involves electroplating (depositing) solid rhodium, Rh(s), onto the surface of the metal from an acidified solution of $Rh_2(SO_4)_3$(aq). Oxygen gas is produced during this process.

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated mass of $Ag_2S$ (may be implicit): 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated mass of $Ag_2S$ (may be implicit): 1 point

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated moles of Ag: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated moles of Ag: 1 point

parts.prompt:
A table of half-reactions related to the overall reaction is provided.

| Half-Reaction | E° (V) |
|--------------|--------|
| $Rh^{3+}$(aq) + 3$e^-$ → Rh(s) | +0.80 |
| $O_2$(g) + 4$H^+$(aq) + 4$e^-$ → 2$H_2O$(l) | +1.23 |

(i) Write the balanced net ionic equation for plating Rh(s) from the acidified $Rh_2(SO_4)_3$(aq) solution.

(ii) Calculate the value of $E°_{cell}$ for the reaction in part (d)(i).

(iii) Based on your answer to part (d)(ii), explain why this process requires the use of an external power source.

scoring_points.description: For the correct balanced equation (state symbols not required): 1 point

required_elements: For the correct balanced equation (state symbols not required): 1 point

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value, consistent with part (d)(i): 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated value, consistent with part (d)(i): 1 point

scoring_points.description: For a correct explanation, consistent with part (d)(ii): 1 point

required_elements: For a correct explanation, consistent with part (d)(ii): 1 point

parts.prompt:
Calculate the length of time, in seconds, required to plate 2.8 g of Rh(s )onto a piece of sterling silver if
2.0 C / s of current is applied.

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value of moles of electrons (may be implicit): 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated value of moles of electrons (may be implicit): 1 point

scoring_points.description: For the correct calculated value of time: 1 point

required_elements: For the correct calculated value of time: 1 point
